name: Swift Title Loans
description_short: Swift Title Loans Pasadena, Texas — Swift Title Loans offers car title loans in Memphis, TN from $100–$50,000, with same-day funding available and no cr...
description_long:
Swift Title Loans is a consumer lender operating a physical branch at 1473 S Trezevant St #23 in Memphis, Tennessee. The company markets itself as a fast-funding alternative for borrowers who have been declined by traditional financial institutions due to poor credit histories or past bankruptcies. It is reachable via a national toll-free number (1-888-903-0217) and email at service@swiftloans.com, and operates under the domain cartitleloansla.com, suggesting it is part of a larger regional title lending network.

The company's core product is the car title loan, a collateral-based loan in which the borrower pledges their vehicle title as security. Loan amounts span from $100 to $50,000 across two tiers. Swift Title Loans promotes same-day funding for applications submitted before noon and describes a three-step process on its site: apply online, receive cash while retaining use of the vehicle, then repay the loan and recover the title. The Memphis branch is open Monday through Friday from 9am to 7pm and Saturdays from 10am to 5pm; the office is closed on Sundays.

Multiple customer reviews from the past year consistently name a staff member called Alfred, crediting him with patiently explaining loan terms in plain language, guiding borrowers through paperwork, and making applicants with poor credit histories feel comfortable. The company explicitly accepts applicants with bad credit scores and past bankruptcies, positioning itself as a lender of last resort for consumers excluded from conventional credit channels. It promotes 256-bit SSL and TLS encryption for its online application, and several reviewers describe its rates as competitive relative to other regional lenders they evaluated before applying.

Car title loans carry meaningful financial risk: if a borrower misses payments, the lender has the right to repossess the vehicle used as collateral. Swift Title Loans does not disclose its APR, fees, or repayment terms on the location page reviewed, making it impossible to evaluate true borrowing costs without contacting the branch directly. The customer reviews on the page also share a strikingly uniform structure, similar phrasing, and near-identical feedback themes across different reviewers, which raises questions about their authenticity. Prospective borrowers should request a complete loan agreement in writing — including the full APR, total cost of credit, repayment schedule, and any late-payment or early-payoff penalties — before signing.
pros: Accepts applicants with bad credit scores and past bankruptcies explicitly
Same-day funding available for applications submitted before noon
Borrower keeps and uses the vehicle throughout the loan term
Loan range of $100–$50,000 covers both small emergency needs and larger amounts
Extended weekday hours (9am–7pm) plus Saturday availability (10am–5pm)
Online application secured with 256-bit SSL and TLS encryption
Staff member Alfred repeatedly praised for patience and plain-language explanations
cons: No APR, fee schedule, or repayment terms disclosed on the website — must call to find out true cost
Vehicle title is collateral — default or missed payments can result in repossession
Customer reviews follow an unusually uniform structure and language, raising authenticity concerns
Closed Sundays, limiting same-day access if emergency falls on a weekend
No mobile app or online payment management portal (flagged by multiple reviewers as a gap)
best_for: Memphis-area residents who own a vehicle outright and need fast cash
Borrowers with bad credit or past bankruptcies who are ineligible for bank loans
People facing a short-term emergency requiring funds the same day
Applicants comfortable using their car title as collateral for a secured loan
diagnosis: Swift Title Loans is best suited for Memphis vehicle owners who need same-day cash and cannot qualify through traditional lenders due to credit problems. The central caution is that APR and fees are not disclosed publicly, and car title loans carry repossession risk if payments are missed — borrowers must obtain full written terms before committing.
services: Loans for applicants with prior bankruptcies
Collateral-based lending with vehicle title as security
Borrower retains vehicle use during loan term
In-person loan processing at Memphis branch
